objectContracts.objectRole: frozen capability-specific rule for deciding whether shadow evidence can support rollout or trust claims
dependencyRules: published requirements must bind publication, runtime, disclosure, and recovery refs
withdrawn requirements force completenessState blocked for dependent capabilities

objectContracts.objectRole: derived completeness result for one capability and shadow dataset cut
dependencyRules: complete requires all required metric families, sample floor, subgroup floor, and current publication tuple
stale is emitted when freshness expires but no hard blocker exists
missing is emitted when dataset, metric, or manifest evidence is absent
blocked is emitted when publication, runtime, disclosure, model, prompt, or recovery posture blocks use

objectContracts.objectRole: addressable metric bundle supporting a completeness assessment
dependencyRules: quarantined or revoked metric sets cannot satisfy complete state
metricSetHash must derive from frozen replay bundle refs
summary exports may reference metric sets but must not reveal raw replay contents by default

objectContracts.objectRole: runtime and publication truth tuple for shadow evidence claims
dependencyRules: blocked or withdrawn publication binding forces shadow completeness blocked
stale publication binding forces shadow completeness stale unless a stricter blocker applies
observe_only may support internal evaluation but cannot imply visible rollout approval

requiredMetricFamilies.meaning: agreement between assistive output and adjudicated expected output
unsupported generated facts, unsafe inference, or fabricated source linkage
clinically or workflow-relevant expected items absent from the output
proportion of output claims backed by frozen evidence spans or structured facts
whether the capability refuses, narrows, or degrades when inputs are unsafe or insufficient
agreement with human-control, escalation, and endpoint-routing policy
visible-use proxy metrics for later feedback comparison without treating edits as trainable by default
coverage of rejected, changed, regenerated, or alternative output paths required for eligibility law
case-slice coverage and disparity checks required before rollout claims
runtime latency, recovery posture, and graceful degradation evidence
proof that evaluation output follows disclosure, artifact-presentation, and export policy
linkage between shadow failures, hazard log entries, and incident review queues

blockedWhen: runtime publication bundle is suspended or revoked
telemetry disclosure fence is missing or stale
release recovery disposition is emergency_kill, hard_block, or rollback_only
assistive evaluation surface binding is blocked or withdrawn
observeOnlyWhen: publication is current but visible approval has not opened
recovery disposition limits the surface to evidence review

completenessDerivationOrder.rule: emit blocked when publication, runtime, disclosure, model, prompt, safety, or recovery posture has a hard blocker
emit missing when the shadow_live manifest, replay bundle cut, required metric family, or publication binding is absent
emit stale when the freshness window has expired or the publication binding is stale and no hard blocker applies
emit complete only when all required metric families, sample floors, subgroup floors, freshness windows, and publication bindings pass

invariants.statement: Shadow evidence can be complete only for the shadow_live partition.
Completeness depends on metrics, freshness, sample floor, subgroup floor, and current runtime-publication truth.
Blocked beats stale, missing, and complete.
Shadow evidence remains invisible to end users and cannot imply live approval.
